nodes.title: Workflow Settings
widgets_values:
You can test and find the best settings yourself. The following table is for reference.

| Parameters | Basic Diffusion | Inpainting |
|--------|---------|------------|
| KSampler: denoise | 1.0 | 0.5 - 1.0 |
| ModelSampling: shift | 3.0 | 3.0 - 10.0 |

nodes.title: 🔍 Core Concept
widgets_values:
This workflow runs **image inpainting** on **Z-Image-Turbo**. The generation stack lives in the **Image Inpainting (Z-Image-Turbo & Fun Union Control)** **subgraph** (Step 2).

## What is local inpainting?

**Local inpainting** means changing **only part of a image** while keeping the rest unchanged.

1. **Load a image** as the starting image.
2. **Draw a mask** on the region to edit (painted area = regenerate, untouched area = keep).
3. **Write a prompt** for what should appear inside the mask.
4. The pipeline builds **inpainting conditioning** from image + mask + prompt — which pixels stay fixed vs. get redrawn.
5. **Sampling** redraws mainly inside the mask; the rest stays aligned with the original and blends at the edges.

Inpainting is **conditional redraw on a masked region**, not full image generation from scratch.

## Why a model patch?

**Z-Image-Turbo** does not support mask-based inpainting on its own. This template uses the **[Fun ControlNet Union](https://huggingface.co/alibaba-pai/Z-Image-Turbo-Fun-Controlnet-Union)** model patch (selected on the subgraph) to enable local edits.

## On this canvas (what you see)

| Step | Group | What to do |
|------|-------|------------|
| 1 | Step 1 — Load Images & Create Mask | Load image, draw and save mask |
| 2 | Step 2 — Update Prompt Here | Set **prompt**, **denoise**, **shift**, and **patch_model** on the subgraph |
| 3 | Result | Save or compare output |

## Where are the other nodes?

**Double-click** the subgraph in Step 2 to open **Image Inpainting (Z-Image-Turbo & Fun Union Control)**.

Nodes such as `InpaintModelConditioning` and `KSampler` are **inside the subgraph only** — they are **Z-Image-Turbo–specific wiring**, not generic inpainting steps. Read the **Inside the subgraph** note there for details.

nodes.title: Subgraph settings
widgets_values:
## Controls on the main canvas

You only see the **subgraph** node here — not the internal sampler or conditioning nodes.

Adjust these **exposed widgets** on the subgraph (Step 2):

- **denoise** (0.5 – 1.0): inpainting strength — lower = subtler edits, higher = stronger redraw inside the mask
- **shift**: ModelSampling shift (see Workflow Settings)
- **patch_model**: Fun ControlNet Union weights

## More detail

**Double-click** the subgraph in Step 2 to open it. See **Inside the subgraph** for how this model wires inpainting (not a generic recipe).

nodes.title: How to create a Mask
widgets_values:
Guide: [maskeditor](https://docs.comfy.org/interface/maskeditor)

## Load Image

1. Load the Image 🌠

## Create Mask

1. **Right-click** on the Load Image node

2. Open in Mask Editor

3. Draw mask over the eyes

4. Save Mask

5. **Hit Run**

nodes.title: Note: Model Links
widgets_values:
Guide: [subgraph](https://docs.comfy.org/interface/features/subgraph)

[Z-Image-Turbo-Fun-Controlnet-Union](https://huggingface.co/alibaba-pai/Z-Image-Turbo-Fun-Controlnet-Union) is used as a **model patch** inside the subgraph. The base model does not support inpainting by itself; this patch enables **local inpainting** (mask + prompt). The same Union family also supports other control types (Canny, HED, Depth, Pose, MLSD) in other workflows.

## Open the subgraph

On the canvas, **double-click** **Image Inpainting (Z-Image-Turbo & Fun Union Control)** in **Step 2 — Update Prompt Here**. Internal loaders, inpaint nodes, and sampling live there. See the **Inside the subgraph** note for Z-Image-Turbo–specific node roles.
Guide: [Subgraph](https://docs.comfy.org/interface/features/subgraph)

## Model Links (for Local Users)

**text_encoders**

- [qwen_3_4b.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/z_image_turbo/resolve/main/split_files/text_encoders/qwen_3_4b.safetensors)

**diffusion_models**

- [z_image_turbo_bf16.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/z_image_turbo/resolve/main/split_files/diffusion_models/z_image_turbo_bf16.safetensors)

**vae**

- [ae.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/z_image_turbo/resolve/main/split_files/vae/ae.safetensors)

**model_patches**

- [Z-Image-Turbo-Fun-Controlnet-Union-2.1.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/alibaba-pai/Z-Image-Turbo-Fun-Controlnet-Union-2.1/resolve/main/Z-Image-Turbo-Fun-Controlnet-Union-2.1.safetensors)


## Model Storage Location

```
📂 ComfyUI/
├── 📂 models/
│   ├── 📂 text_encoders/
│   │   └── qwen_3_4b.safetensors
│   ├── 📂 diffusion_models/
│   │   └── z_image_turbo_bf16.safetensors
│   ├── 📂 vae/
│   │   └── ae.safetensors
│   └── 📂 model_patches/
│       └── Z-Image-Turbo-Fun-Controlnet-Union-2.1.safetensors
```

## Report Issue

Note: Please update ComfyUI first ([guide](https://docs.comfy.org/installation/update_comfyui)) and prepare required models. Desktop/Cloud updates follow stable releases, so some nightly-supported models may not be available yet.

- Cannot run / runtime errors: [ComfyUI/issues](https://github.com/comfyanonymous/ComfyUI/issues)
- UI / frontend issues: [ComfyUI_frontend/issues](https://github.com/Comfy-Org/ComfyUI_frontend/issues)
- Workflow issues: [workflow_templates/issues](https://github.com/Comfy-Org/workflow_templates/issues)

nodes.title: Inside the subgraph
widgets_values:
You are inside **Image Inpainting (Z-Image-Turbo & Fun Union Control)**.

This wiring is **specific to Z-Image-Turbo + Fun ControlNet Union** — not a universal inpainting template. On the main canvas you only see the subgraph; open this view via **double-click** on that node in **Step 2**.

## Groups on this graph

| Group | Role |
|-------|------|
| **Model** | Load Z-Image-Turbo, CLIP, VAE, and the Union **model patch** |
| **Prompt** | CLIP encode your edit prompt |
| **Fun ControlNet** | `ZImageFunControlnet` — applies the patch so the base model can run inpaint-aware diffusion |
| *(right side)* | `InpaintModelConditioning` + `KSampler` + decode |

## Key nodes (Z-Image-Turbo only)

- **`InpaintModelConditioning`** — image + mask + prompt → inpaint conditioning (masked latent, positive/negative)
- **`ZImageFunControlnet`** — connects base model, patch, VAE, inpaint image, and mask
- **`ModelSamplingAuraFlow`** — **shift** (exposed on subgraph)
- **`KSampler`** — denoising; **denoise** (exposed on subgraph) sets inpainting strength

## Back to the main canvas

Press `Esc` to back to main canvas

Close the subgraph editor to return. Use **Step 1** for image/mask, **Step 2** widgets for prompt and settings, **Result** to save or compare.
